summary: Test suite violates core requirement: task explicitly states 'ALL TESTS MUST RUN THROUGH ACT' and 'Do NOT test your script directly,' but check_licenses.bats invokes the script directly with local fixtures. workflow.bats is well-designed with clever shared state to avoid re-running act, but covers only 2 test cases. Missing critical coverage: malformed manifests, empty/unusual version strings, unusual dependency names, empty allow/deny lists, workflow failure modes, and requirements.txt through act pipeline. Main happy paths (manifest parsing, classification, exit codes) are tested, but architectural violation (direct script testing vs. all-through-act) is fundamental.
